sentence: The new advertising campaign was very _____ .
explanation: EFFECT → effective (add -ive to make an adjective meaning 'having an effect')

sentence: She gave a very _____ speech at the conference.
explanation: PERSUADE → persuasive (change -de to -sive to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ nature of the chemical made it dangerous.
explanation: EXPLODE → explosive (change -de to -sive to make an adjective)

sentence: His _____ behaviour at the party embarrassed everyone.
explanation: OUTRAGE → outrageous (add -ous to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the new employee was remarkable.
explanation: COMPETENT → competence (change -t to -ce to make a noun)

sentence: She showed great _____ in handling the crisis.
explanation: CONFIDENT → confidence (change -t to -ce to make a noun)

sentence: The _____ landscape of the mountains was breathtaking.
explanation: MOUNTAIN → mountainous (add -ous to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the new technology was impressive.
explanation: INNOVATE → innovation (change -e to -ion to make a noun)

sentence: His _____ approach to problem-solving was admired.
explanation: CREATE → creative (change -e to -ive to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the old building was a major concern.
explanation: MAINTAIN → maintenance (change -tain to -tenance to make a noun)

sentence: She received a _____ response from the audience.
explanation: FAVOUR → favourable (add -able to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the new drug was tested in clinical trials.
explanation: SAFE → safety (change -e to -ety to make a noun)

sentence: His _____ remarks offended many people.
explanation: PROVOKE → provocative (change -ke to -cative to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the project depended on good teamwork.
explanation: SUCCEED → success (change -eed to -ess to make a noun)

sentence: She made a _____ decision that changed her career.
explanation: COURAGE → courageous (add -ous to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the evidence was questioned by the defence.
explanation: RELEVANT → relevance (change -t to -ce to make a noun)

sentence: The company adopted a more _____ strategy.
explanation: COMPETE → competitive (change -e to -itive to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the new policy was debated in parliament.
explanation: IMPLEMENT → implementation (add -ation to make a noun)

sentence: She has an _____ personality that attracts people.
explanation: ATTRACT → attractive (add -ive to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the old system was no longer acceptable.
explanation: DEPEND → dependence (add -ence to make a noun)

sentence: His _____ attitude made him popular with colleagues.
explanation: COOPERATE → cooperative (change -e to -ive to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the new museum attracted many visitors.
explanation: MAGNIFICENT → magnificence (change -t to -ce to make a noun)

sentence: She gave a _____ performance in the play.
explanation: MARVEL → marvellous (add -lous to make an adjective)

sentence: The _____ of the new employee exceeded expectations.
explanation: PERFORM → performance (add -ance to make a noun)

sentence: His _____ comments during the meeting were unhelpful.
explanation: DESTRUCT → destructive (add -ive to make an adjective)
